title: Choose FFT length and radix for the workload
description: Select FFT size as a power of two near the signal length and pick a radix the target hardware accelerates, padding rather than truncating.
body: Use when transforming a finite signal block and you control the transform length. Do not truncate the input to force a convenient size, since dropped samples discard energy and shift bins. Actions: round the block length up to a power of two, zero-pad to that length, and verify the chosen radix maps onto the platform's FFT primitive. Validation signals include a parity test where inverse FFT reconstructs the padded input and bin resolution equals sample_rate divided by FFT length. Risk boundary: when latency forbids padding, escalate to a chirp-z or Bluestein transform rather than silently dropping samples.
